original_abstract:
In the current digital era, higher education institutions are increasingly reliant on e-learning platforms to provide flexible and accessible learning environments. This study evaluates the quality of the e-learning portal at Universitas Mahakarya Asia (elearning.unmaha.ac.id) using the McCall Quality Model. This framework provides a systematic approach to software evaluation by examining three primary dimensions: product operations, product revision, and product transition. Specifically, this research assesses six critical factors: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability. Data were gathered through structured questionnaires and analyzed using weighted performance formulas to determine a final quality percentage.

The evaluation yielded an overall quality score of 79.6%, placing the platform in the "Good" category. Detailed analysis revealed that while the system performs exceptionally well in terms of integrity (94%), reliability (92%), and efficiency (88%), there are notable areas for improvement. Correctness scored 71.8% and usability reached 75%, while maintainability received the lowest rating at 62.6%. These findings suggest that while the platform is secure and dependable, emphasis should be placed on refining the user interface, improving the accuracy of information, and streamlining technical maintenance processes. This study provides a foundational roadmap for the university to enhance its digital learning infrastructure and ensures a more responsive experience for its academic community.
humanized_abstract:
As the new age of technology develops the Higher Education Institutions (HEI) take advantage of the on-line services (e-learning platforms) provided by the information and communication technologies to undertake their educational activities. Quality assurance assessment of an on-line service is a must for the purposes of assessment and improvement. In the literature various models and approaches are brought up for assessing the quality of the services. In this study the software Quality Model developed by McCall is used for assessing the quality of Universitas Mahakarya Asia on-line learning portal which is named as elearning.unmaha.ac.id. This model examines three major categories of the quality criteria; product operations, product revision and product transition. In this study within these main categories, the six main factors such as correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability are analyzed. Information required for assessment of the software quality criteria are obtained from the answers of questionnaires prepared according to McCall model. The obtained data from questionnaires are analyzed using weighted performance formula and the quality of e-learning portal is obtained as the quality percent.

EVALUACIÓN DE LOS SERVICIOS DE PLATFORMA, S.L. El resultado final de la evaluación ha dado como resultado una puntuación global del 79,6% por lo que categorizaría al nivel de Servicios de “Buena” calidad. La plataforma destaca en grandes aspectos, en la categoría de la integridad del sistema obtuvo un 94% de puntúa y en el caso de los aspectos de fiabilidad y celeridad en los accesos, alcanzó un 92% y un 88% respectivamente. Por contra, en la precisión en la información dada, obtuvo un 71,8% en esta evaluación. La plataforma en cuanto a la categoría de usabilidad, alcanzó en esta ocasión el 75% y a la hora de analizar la mantenibilidad del sistema o funcionalidades obtenido el 62,6% más bajos de las categorías evaluadas. Por lo que, la plataforma de recursos virtuales del sistema de aprendizaje de la universidad, ofrece unos servicios ampliamente garantizados y seguros, y que, por lo general los usuarios están enterados en tiempo y formas adecuadas de los problemas y afectaciones que, a la hora de utilizar la plataforma, pudieran surgir. Por lo que, y en términos generales, que el desarrollo que tiene el sistema de aprendizaje, ofrece una bastante ajustada y funcionable. Esta evaluación constituye un punto de partida fundamental para el desarrollo a futuro de los sistemas de aprendizaje para que todos los recursos virtuales en la universidad contribuyan al entorno formativo más óptimo posible y para que los usuarios tengan el acceso más adecuado al tiempo que mejores experiencias en el uso del sistema.
